title: Construct ERC6551/clone init code in memory by laying out fields backwards from a chosen pivot offset
trigger_patterns: minimal proxy init code in assembly
backward layout for keccak256 over init code
description: ERC6551 clone init code embeds the implementation address, salt, chainId, tokenContract, tokenId at fixed offsets. Solady writes these fields backwards starting from the highest offset (tokenId at 0xa3) so that simpler mstore writes can overlap and overwrite the head bytes with the canonical proxy prefix 0x3d60ad80600a3d3981f3363d3d373d3d3d363d73 last. Then keccak256(start, 0xb7) hashes exactly the init code. Backwards layout avoids the need to compute exact non-overlapping offsets.
when_to_apply: Computing CREATE2 addresses for ERC6551 clones, minimal proxies with appended args (LibClone), or any deterministic deployment with structured init code
when_not_to_apply: General-purpose init code construction where backwards layout obscures intent
